title: Replace Incremental IDs with GUIDs in User-Facing URLs
description:
## Context
Incremental (auto-increment) IDs are exposed in URLs and UI. In a multi-tenant pooled database, this poses a security risk (enumeration attacks) and is flagged by ISO 27001 requirements.

## Change
Replace all user-facing incremental IDs (particularly `view_id`) with GUIDs. This affects:
- Dictionary views
- Migration views
- Entity Relationship Diagram views
- Options and objects associated with each diagram

## Impact
Significant testing required as many features rely on `view_id` for routing and data association.
acceptance_criteria: All user-facing URLs use GUIDs instead of incremental IDs
Dictionary, Migration, and ERD views use GUID-based routing
No incremental IDs are exposed in the UI or URLs
All existing features continue to work with GUID-based IDs
ux_changes: URLs and UI no longer show incremental IDs — replaced with GUIDs.
middleware_changes: Route resolution and data lookup updated to use GUIDs instead of incremental IDs.
data_model_changes: GUID columns added alongside or replacing incremental ID references in view-related tables.
api_changes: All endpoints that accept view_id now accept GUID format.
implementation_notes: Large change touching dictionary, migration, ERD, and associated options/objects. Extensive testing needed before merging to dev. GUIDs prevent enumeration attacks in the multi-tenant pooled database.
comment_summary: Developer completed development but emphasized thorough testing is needed before merging since many features rely on view_id. Deployed to dev for review.
